column2: A company is concerned that someone could read locally stored data from a stolen smartphone. Which mobile-device hardening technique directly addresses this risk?
column5: A screen lock controls normal access to the device interface. Encryption adds protection for the stored data when an unauthorized person has physical possession of the device.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield Encryption DataProtection Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user leaves a phone unattended on a desk. Which hardening control should require authentication before another person can use the device interface?
column5: The lock can use an approved method such as a PIN, biometric, or pattern. Device encryption serves a related but different purpose by protecting stored data.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield ScreenLock AccessControl Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which two screen-lock methods listed in Objective 2.8 authenticate by a user's biometric traits?
column5: A PIN or pattern depends on information the user provides. Facial and fingerprint recognition instead measure a physical characteristic.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic Biometrics ScreenLock Recognition Source::Messer-v140

column2: An administrator must apply one predefined set of email and device settings to every phone in a department. What should the administrator assign through the management platform?
column5: A profile packages a consistent group of settings for distribution to many devices. This makes the setup easier to manage consistently than configuring each phone individually.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield ConfigurationProfiles MDM Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: An organization needs one central system to manage mobile-device profiles, security controls, and application use. Which technology fits this need?
column5: MDM is the centralized management layer. A configuration profile is one bundle of settings that the management system can deploy.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield MDM CentralizedManagement Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A phone's applications are current, but the mobile operating system is missing security fixes. Which patch-management action is required?
column5: Application updates do not replace operating-system patches. Mobile patch management must keep both layers current.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield PatchManagement OSUpdates Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A phone's operating system is current, but one installed app is an outdated version with known security problems. Which patch-management action addresses that exposure?
column5: An OS update does not update every installed application. Each software layer needs its corresponding updates.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield PatchManagement ApplicationUpdates Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which category of mobile endpoint software is intended to detect or block malicious software?
column5: Content filtering restricts access to selected sites or applications. It does not serve the same malware-detection role.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield Antivirus AntiMalware EndpointSecurity Source::Messer-v140

column2: A company wants managed phones to block access to selected websites and applications. Which endpoint security control should it use?
column5: Filtering enforces access rules based on content or destination. Antivirus and anti-malware instead focus on malicious software.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield ContentFiltering EndpointSecurity Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A phone is misplaced nearby, and the owner wants to view its location or make it produce a sound. Which mobile security feature should be used?
column5: Location and alert functions help recover the hardware. They do not erase its data or create a recoverable copy of that data.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic Locator LostDevice Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A stolen corporate phone is unlikely to be recovered, and its local data must be removed from a distance. Which action directly meets this requirement?
column5: A locator may help find a device, while a remote backup preserves a copy of its data. Remote wipe is the control that deletes data from the device.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield RemoteWipe DataProtection Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user must be able to restore mobile data to a replacement phone after the original is lost or damaged. Which mobile security method provides that recovery capability?
column5: Backup preserves a separate recoverable copy. Encryption protects local data, and remote wipe removes it, but neither control creates the copy needed for restoration.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield RemoteBackup Recovery Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: An attacker repeatedly guesses a phone's unlock code. Which mobile policy control directly limits continued guessing?
column5: The configured response may lock the device, require additional account authentication, or wipe data. The exact threshold and response should follow policy rather than an assumed universal value.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield FailedLogins AccessControl Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Before applying mobile management controls, which ownership distinction should an organization's policy define?
column4: Whether the device is bring your own device (BYOD) or corporate-owned.
column5: The policy establishes which devices are managed and what management scope applies. MDM then provides the centralized mechanism for enforcing supported controls.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield BYOD CorporateOwned Policy Source::Messer-v140

column2: An organization requires every managed phone to use encryption and an approved screen lock. Which policy element defines these mandatory controls?
column5: These requirements state the approved security controls. A configuration profile can package the settings so the MDM system can apply them consistently.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 44
column10: A+::220-1202::2.8 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::MobileDeviceSecurity Basic HighYield ConfigurationProfiles SecurityRequirements Scenario Source::Messer-v140
